title: Understanding Data Quality Dimensions
text: Data quality dimensions help us evaluate the reliability and usability of data. Consider what it means for data to be consistent. How does this relate to the expected number of rows?

title: Analyzing the Row Count Discrepancy
text: Think about why having $$50000$$ rows instead of the expected $$10000$$ might be problematic. What does this tell you about the data's reliability?

title: Consistency in Data
text: Consistency refers to the uniformity and reliability of data across different datasets or time periods. How does this apply to the row count issue?

title: Identifying the Correct Data Quality Dimension
text: Based on the hints provided, which data quality dimension is most likely affected by the unexpected row count?
